evals.prompt: Use the doca-flow-dpa-provider skill to help me. I'm exporting a doca-flow pipe and external resources into BlueField DPA address space so a DPACC kernel can read counters and mutate hash-pipe entries — walking per-port doca_flow_dpa_ctx, the three queue types, and the export handshake (_export_prepare -> add entries -> _export -> _get_device_addr).
evals.expected_output: The agent loads doca-flow-dpa-provider and answers from the documented export workflow — per-port doca_flow_dpa_ctx, the general/resources-write/resources-read queue types, and the order-sensitive _export_prepare -> add entries -> _export -> _get_device_addr handshake — quoting only real symbols.
assertions: The doca-flow-dpa-provider skill is selected and its SKILL.md/TASKS.md drive the answer.
The order-sensitive export handshake and the three queue types are used correctly.
Every symbol named is traceable to the bundle (no invented tokens).

evals.prompt: I'm running into this: "my DPA kernel never sees the entries in the exported pipe". Can you walk me through it?
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes this as a doca-flow-dpa-provider export-ordering issue even though the skill is not named, loads it, and walks the export handshake sequencing grounded in the bundle.
assertions: The doca-flow-dpa-provider skill is triggered from the implicit phrasing without the user naming it.
The answer ties the missing entries to the _export_prepare/add/_export/_get_device_addr ordering.

evals.prompt: I'm running into this: "BAD_STATE from _pipe_export, and I also want to disable a hash entry from inside a DPA kernel". Can you walk me through it?
evals.expected_output: The agent loads doca-flow-dpa-provider and diagnoses the BAD_STATE export path and the DPA-side entry-mutation API from the bundle, grounded in real symbols.
assertions: The doca-flow-dpa-provider skill is triggered from the implicit phrasing without the user naming it.
The answer explains the export state machine and DPA-side hash-entry mutation.

evals.prompt: I just need to construct a doca-flow pipe, and separately do generic host-side DPA context work. Which skill should handle this and why is it not doca-flow-dpa-provider?
evals.expected_output: The agent does NOT force doca-flow-dpa-provider; it routes pipe construction to doca-flow, generic host-side DPA to doca-dpa, and DPA-side kernel-writing out, explaining this skill only covers the Flow->DPA export bridge.
assertions: doca-flow-dpa-provider is not used as the primary skill for this out-of-scope request.
The answer routes to the appropriate skill(s): doca-flow / doca-dpa.
